response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — including hands-on IDE development at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and authoring test frameworks — but the core required stack is largely absent. There is no Kotlin experience, no Spring or enterprise Java framework background, and Java appears only peripherally in older roles. Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and D, which does not meet the JD's hard requirement for deep JVM, Java/Kotlin, and Spring ecosystem expertise.
strengths: Direct IDE-development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Built language tooling from scratch (D test runner, assertion library, web server) showing genuine interest in developer tools
Strong craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems, and community teaching/workshops
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise-framework experience, both hard requirements
Java exposure is minor, dated (2016-2018), and not on large-scale JVM codebases
Recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node, D, full-stack product work) diverges from JVM tooling focus
